etymology.description: 紀(紀) (jì, "record") expresses keeping memory or events in a form of writing; 念(念) (niàn, "thought"; "remember") reflects holding memories in the mind; 品(品) (pǐn, "item"; "product") indicates a tangible object. Together, this character combination forms the concept of a "紀念品(紀念品)," referring to an object that helps record and remember past experiences.
etymology.interpretation: 紀(紀) (jì, "record") + 念(念) (niàn, "thought"; "remember") creates the context of calling to mind or preserving something mentally. With 品(品) (pǐn, "item") added, it signifies an item that concretizes a memory, thereby creating a "souvenir" or "keepsake." This combination makes sense because a "纪念品(紀念品)" is a tangible object designed to physically embody or trigger the memory of an event, maintaining its recall over time.

examples.chinese: 我在博物馆买了纪念品(我在博物館買了紀念品)。
examples.english: I bought a souvenir at the museum.

examples.chinese: 旅行结束时(旅行結束時)，我带回了许多纪念品(我帶回了許多紀念品)。
examples.pinyin: Lǚxíng jiéshù shí, wǒ dài huíle xǔduō jìniànpǐn.
examples.english: When the trip ended, I brought back many souvenirs.

examples.chinese: 这个杯子是我从巴黎带回来的纪念品(這個杯子是我從巴黎帶回來的紀念品)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège bēizi shì wǒ cóng Bālí dài huílái de jìniànpǐn.
examples.english: This cup is a souvenir I brought back from Paris.
